item.title: Remote Working and Cybersecurity Risk
item.description: Remote work has a great number of benefits, but also comes with significant cybersecurity issues that should be addressed.

item.title: How Cymulate Assists With ISO 27001 Certification and Compliance
item.description: The rise in data breaches, cyber attacks, nation-state hacking incidents and ransomware outbreaks has led many global companies, enterprises and governments to comply with standards like ISO 27001 to keep their information assets secure. However, they often don’t know how effective their data and system security really is.

item.title: BAS 101: What is Email Gateway Attack Simulation? - Cymulate
item.description: What is an email gateway attack simulation? How is that different from phishing testing?” Both are good questions, let’s have a look.

item.title: ChatGPT and Data Leakage: The Hidden Risks of AI-Powered Conversations
item.description: Data leakage (a form of data exfiltration) is not a new topic in the cybersecurity world. ChatGPT is just making it faster. Learn more here.

item.title: What You Need to Know About PrintNightmare Vulnerability - Cymulate
item.description: This tech-blog post demonstrates how PrintNightmare (CVE-2021-34527) attack is implemented in the Cymulate's Continuous Security Validation.

item.title: What is Continuous Security Validation? - Cymulate
item.description: Is your cybersecurity keeping pace with evolving threats? Learn how Continuous Security Validation strengthens your defenses and prevents breaches 24/7

item.title: Have You Noticed? There Are More Critical Vulnerabilities Than log4j
item.description: Read how the new Microsoft vulnerabilities are being exploited to access active directories and how they can be remediated.

item.title: From Content Overload to Actionable Security: The Power of Context
item.description: Security data overload can hinder decision-making. Learn how prioritization, urgency, and achievability turn content into actionable cybersecurity insights

item.title: Introducing Cymulate Yearly Platform Usage Report
item.description: Discover how Cymulate Security Validation minimizes risks and strengthens enterprise defenses with proven results from customer data

item.title: Security Segmentation Validation in the Banking Industry
item.description: Explore how lateral movement threats impact banking cybersecurity and how Cymulate’s simulations help identify and mitigate network segmentation risks

item.title: How Do You Know? Answers to help the board understand the value of your security program
item.description: Learn how Continuous Threat Exposure Management (CTEM) enables CISOs to communicate risk, visibility, and protection with clarity and confidence

item.title: Cyber Threat Breakdown September 2023
item.description: What were the most critical cyber threats last month? Here is a summary of cybercriminal activities in September 2023.

item.title: Cyber Threat Breakdown October 2023
item.description: What were the most critical cyber threats last month? Here is a summary of cybercriminal activities in October 2023.

item.title: Cymulate’s January 2022 Cyberattacks Wrap-up
item.description: What were the important cyberattacks last month? Here is a summary of cybercriminal activity in January 2022.

item.title: Cymulate’s March 2021 Cyberattacks Wrap-up
item.description: What were the important cyberattacks last month? Here is a summary of cybercriminal activity in March 2021.

item.title: Cybercrooks are Laughing All the Way from the Bank - Cymulate
item.description: American financial institutions are victimized at a staggering 1 billion times per year. Recovering from an attack is costly, with the latest estimations calculating the price of $18 million USD each institution. These institutions can validate their cybersecurity posture themselves with Cymulate.

item.title: Ransomware’s Deadly Impact: Lessons from a Tragic Hospital Attack
item.description: A ransomware attack on a German hospital forced ER closures, delaying critical care and tragically causing a patient’s death.

item.title: Massive Data Hack Becomes Personal for German Politicians
item.description: A major data breach was carried out against German politicians by a 20-year old man using the pseudonyms “G0t” and “Orbit”. His reasons? Anger at politicians.

item.title: What Do I Need to Know About the SolarWinds Attack?
item.description: SolarWinds fell victim to a highly sophisticated attack that left their customers vulnerable to further threats. Let's look at the situation.

item.title: Creating a Full Kill Chain Attack With MITRE's Engenuity Evaluation APTs
item.description: Take a deep dive into MITRE Engenuity's latest attack simulations. Learn how they work and how you can use the data to keep safe.

item.title: Lapsus$, Okta, Microsoft, and RDP – The Attack Success Factors
item.description: Get up to speed on the alarming reach of the Lapsus$ threat actor group as they gained access to the sensitive data of multiple enterprises.

item.title: How Continuous Security Validation Improves Collaboration
item.description: This blog shares how Continuous Security Validation technology can help improve and build collaborative relationships within risk management.

item.title: How to Leverage Log4j to Expose vCenter Attack Paths - Part 1
item.description: A guide to exposing a vCenter attack path using Log4j. Read more to get a step-by-step explanation of how this can help.

item.title: What Do Customers Say About Cymulate?
item.description: What do customers love about Cymulate? We spoke to some at our annual SKO and learned a lot from the security experts who spoke.

item.title: Dynamic Dashboards and Reporting in the MSSP World
item.description: Learn why Cymulate's Dynamic Dashboards are an essential requirement for MSSPs day-to-day operations in this blog post.

item.title: Messing with CVE-2022-30190 by Understanding Compound File Binary Format and OLE Structures
item.description: Gain an understanding of the Compound File Binary Format and OLE Structures to Mess with CVE-2022-30190 in this blog post by Cymulate.

item.title: Trouble on the Horizon - Spring CVE
item.description: Take a lesson from the Log4j debacle and update to a patched version of the Spring CVE library to prevent cyberattacks.

item.title: PCI Compliance with Cymulate - Keep Payment Data Safe
item.description: The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) ensures that any organization handling credit card information keeps data safe.

item.title: Iran’s Growing Role in Cyber warfare
item.description: Regardless of where you work, you are either aware of Iranian involvement in cyber warfare or have experienced it firsthand.

item.title: Are You Ready for the Next Cyber-Attack? - Cymulate
item.description: To prepare for cyber attacks, organizations need to think and act like real threat actors, using a wide range of malware tools and attack techniques

item.title: Simulating Cyber Attacks vs. Cyber Attack Techniques Methods
item.description: Should you simulate specific cyberattacks or run simulations based on cyberattack methods? Which method is best for your organization?

item.title: Security Must Adapt to Continuous Change
item.description: Technology and security professionals must overcome collective inertia and adapt to security's continuously changing demands.

item.title: Cyber 101: When Ransomware Kills
item.description: Unfortunately, the answer to 'Has ransomware ever killed anyone?' is 'yes'. Read to understand the deadly implications of cyberattacks.

item.title: Chinese APT Attack On-Premises Version of Microsoft Exchange
item.description: In this blog, we Analyze the Chinese APT Attack Affecting 60,000+ Companies Running On-Premises Version of Microsoft Exchange.

item.title: Is Your Organization GDPR Ready?
item.description: GDPR is the brainchild of ENISA to stem the increasing number of reported data breaches, especially those relating to online systems and services

item.title: GDPR Privacy Anyone? - Cymulate XSPM
item.description: Authorities take GDPR privacy very seriously., leading to a £120,000 fine over a data breach that exposed 20,000 staff and students

item.title: CVE-2022-30190 aka Follina - Verify Your Exposure
item.description: Rated a 7.8 high CVSS 3.x severity base score, CVE-2022-30190 takes advantage of the MSDT, an official tool built-in all versions of Windows.

item.title: CVE-2022-30190, AKA Follina, Uses Macro-less Word Docs to Drop RCE Files
item.description: Rated a 7.8 high CVSS 3.x severity base score, CVE-2022-30190 takes advantage of the MSDT, an official tool built-in all versions of Windows.

item.title: Cyber Threat Breakdown June 2023
item.description: What were the most critical cyber threats last month? Here is a summary of cybercriminal activities in July 2023.

item.title: What CopyKittens Can Teach Us About Cyber-security
item.description: Cyber security lessons to learn from the CopyKittens professional cyber ring attacks and their malware distribution techniques.

item.title: The Evolution and Outlook of Exposure Management - Cymulate
item.description: Learn how exposure management evolved from finance to cybersecurity, tackling modern risks, in this CyberWire podcast with Avihai Ben-Yossef.

item.title: Full-Context Cyber Threat Exposure Management Explained
item.description: Full-context CTEM gives security teams actionable insights into their exposure landscape, enabling effective responses to evolving cyber threats

item.title: Closing Out the CTEM Journey with Validation and Mobilization
item.description: CTEM helps organizations understand their needs from both a technical and a business perspective. See the step-by-step process in this blog.

item.title: The Technology Behind the Cymulate AI Copilot
item.description: Discover how Cymulate's AI Copilot and Dynamic Attack Planner revolutionize attack simulations, enhancing efficiency and ease for security professionals

item.title: The Quickest Way to Comply with NIST Revision 5
item.description: Learn how to optimize your cybersecurity ROI by complying with the New NIST (SP) 800-53A, Revision 5 Standards in this blog from Cymulate.

item.title: Iranian Hacker Group OilRig Keeps On Cyber Drilling
item.description: Iranian hacker group OilRig, aka PT34 or Helix Kitten, poses a persistent threat to technology services providers and a government entities

item.title: Why Cyber Security Risk is Never Worth the Price - Cymulate
item.description: This blog post demonstrates why Cyber Security Risk is Never Worth the Price and why it's important to Think independently before deciding.

item.title: Cyber Attacks Wrap Up - September 2020
item.description: What were the notable cyber attacks last month? Here is a rundown of cybercriminal activity in September 2020.

item.title: Kubernetes Security Best Practices: Insights from Real-World Attack Simulations
item.description: From RBAC exploits to container escapes, real-world attacks reveal cloud-native detection blind spots. Get best practices to reduce Kubernetes risk.

item.title: How to Approach the Demanding 23 NYCRR 500 Regulation
item.description: In March 2017, the New York State Department of Financial Services (NYDFS) issued a new regulation, the much discussed 23 NYCRR part 500. Considered to be one of the harshest cybersecurity regulations ever to impact companies, it consists of a new set of standards and requirements for banks, insurance companies, and other financial services organizations.
